data.title: Key facts: Berkshire Hathaway increases Chubb stake; CEO letter set for 2026
data.description:
• Berkshire Hathaway boosted its stake in Chubb, acquiring four million shares in Q3, totaling 31 million shares worth over $9 billion.1
  • Berkshire Hathaway will publish CEO Greg Abel's first annual letter on February 28, 2026, at 8 a.m. Eastern, alongside fourth-quarter results and the 2025 annual report.2

data.title: Key facts: Arista Networks launches new products; profit growth reported; analysts raise price targets
data.description:
• In 2025, Arista Networks launched Cluster Load Balancing and CloudVision® Universal Network Observability™, acquired VeloCloud® SD-WAN from Broadcom, and added key executives.1
  • Arista Networks showed profit growth due to AI networking advancements and new products like the R4 series and Arista AVA, boosting network performance and security.2
  • Several financial firms have raised their price targets for Arista Networks (ANET), with estimates ranging from $178 to $200, indicating a positive shift in analyst outlook.3

data.title: Key facts: Ares Management Q4 revenues rise 23.4%; debt on Lyonnais cut
data.description:
• Ares Management (ARES) posted Q4 revenues of $1.52 billion, up 23.4% year-over-year, but missed forecasts by 7%, causing a 1.1% drop in stock price to $135.75.1
  • Ares Management has reduced the debt tied to Olympique Lyonnais to about 32 cents on the dollar, after recovering over $200 million from Eagle's Crystal Palace stake sale.2

data.title: Key facts: American Airlines plans Venezuela flights; Senators criticize FAA
data.description:
• American Airlines seeks approval to resume flights from Miami to Venezuela's Caracas and Maracaibo, following a lifted ban, marking a potential return after over six years.1
  • U.S. Senators criticized the FAA for its failures in a January 2025 collision involving an American Airlines jet and an Army helicopter, resulting in 67 fatalities near Reagan National Airport.2

data.title: Key facts: Amazon's stock drops; plans $200B AI investment by 2026; faces conglomerate discount
data.description:
• Amazon's stock has fallen amid talks of a long-term reevaluation or short-term adjustments due to execution risks and rising costs. AI investments may boost future gains but hurt short-term profits.1
  • In 2026, Amazon plans to invest $200 billion, leading hyperscalers in AI capital spending, part of a total $650 billion projected by Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, and Alphabet.2
  • Amazon may face a 'conglomerate discount' like General Electric, as its growing diversification could result in lower valuations compared to more focused rivals.3

data.title: Key facts: Alphabet's $180B AI Investment; Appeals Court Rules on Patents; Berkshire's Q4 May Show Alphabet Stake Changes
data.description:
• In 2025, Alphabet invested $180 billion in AI data centers, with spending expected to rise in 2026. This will significantly impact financials, with depreciation costs likely to double.1
  • A U.S. appeals court upheld a rule limiting patent-validity reviews, giving judges more power to deny inter partes review petitions, affecting Google's patent strategy.2
  • Berkshire Hathaway's Q4 filing may reveal changes in its Alphabet (GOOG) stake, after acquiring nearly 18 million shares at $210, now valued at about $5.5 billion.3

data.title: Key facts: Airbnb Q4 profit drops; revenue up 12% to $2.78 billion; new booking option launched
data.description:
• Airbnb reported Q4 profit of $341 million, down from $461 million. Revenue rose 12% to $2.78 billion, exceeding forecasts. Q1 2026 revenue guidance is $2.59-$2.63 billion.123
  • Airbnb is partnering with boutique hotels in cities like New York and Los Angeles to boost its market reach and attract more guests.45
  • Airbnb introduced 'Reserve Now, Pay Later' in the U.S., allowing guests to book without upfront payment, boosting fourth-quarter bookings.6

data.title: Key facts: AMD's Q1 revenue from China misses expectations; shares drop 13%
data.description:
• AMD's February 3 earnings showed $390 million in revenue from China, missing investor expectations and causing shares to drop over 13%.1
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) received a Neutral rating from DA Davidson, with a $220 price target, amid concerns over its competitive position in the AI accelerator market.2

data.title: Key facts: AIG Files Form 10-K; Wells Fargo Raises Price Target to $87
data.description:
• American International Group (AIG) has submitted its Form 10-K to Euronext Dublin, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance and operational activities for the previous year.1

data.title: US natural gas futures climb with increased LNG export flows
data.description:
Key points:
  • Near-record LNG export flows boost demand
  • Waha Hub prices remain negative due to pipeline constraints
  • LSEG reports increased gas output in Lower 48 states

By Scott DiSavino

U.S. natural gas futures edged up to a one-week high on Friday before the long U.S. Presidents' Day weekend on near-record flows of gas to liquefied natural gas export plants and forecasts for more demand this week than previously expected.

That small price increase came despite forecasts for the weather to remain warmer than normal through the end of February, which should keep heating demand low and allow energy firms to pull less gas from storage than usual over the next couple of weeks.

Gas futures for March delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange rose 2.6 cents, or 0.8%, to settle at $3.243 per million British thermal units, their highest close since February 6.

That put the contract down about 5% for the week after it lost about 21% last week and 17% two weeks ago.

In the cash market, average prices at the Waha Hub (NG-WAH-WTX-SNL) in the Permian Basin in West Texas remained in negative territory for a seventh day in a row and the 16th time this year, as pipeline constraints trapped gas in the nation's biggest oil-producing basin.

Daily Waha prices first fell below zero in 2019. They did so 17 times in 2019, six times in 2020, once in 2023, a record 49 times in 2024, and 39 times in 2025.

Waha prices have averaged $1.33 per mmBtu so far this year, compared with $1.15 in 2025 and a five-year average (2021-2025) of $2.88.

SUPPLY AND DEMAND

Financial firm LSEG said average gas output in the Lower 48 states climbed to 107.8 billion cubic feet per day so far in February, up from 106.3 bcfd in January. That compares with a monthly record high of 109.7 bcfd in December.

After extreme cold over the past couple of weeks, meteorologists projected weather across much of the country would remain mostly warmer than normal through at least February 28.

If those forecasts are correct, analysts expect heating demand will remain lower than normal through the end of February, which should allow energy firms to leave more gas in storage than usual, wiping out the current inventory deficit by early March. There is currently about 6% less gas in stockpiles than usual for this time of year.

LSEG projected average gas demand in the Lower 48 states, including exports, would fall from 141.7 bcfd this week to 123.9 bcfd next week before rising to 131.9 bcfd in two weeks. The forecast for this week was higher than LSEG's outlook on Thursday.

Average gas flows to the eight large U.S. LNG export plants rose to 18.5 bcfd so far in February, up from 17.8 bcfd in January. That compares with a monthly record high of 18.5 bcfd in December.

data.title: GROUP 1 AUTOMOTIVE INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Group 1 Automotive Inc., a leading player in the automotive retail industry, has released its annual 10-K report, showcasing a robust financial performance and strategic initiatives aimed at sustaining growth. The report highlights significant increases in revenue and gross profit, alongside strategic acquisitions and investments in technology to enhance operational efficiency. The company also outlines the challenges and risks it faces in a competitive market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $22,571.4 million, a 13.2% increase compared to the prior year, driven by higher revenues across all business lines.
  • Gross Profit: $3,621.8 million, an 11.8% increase from the previous year, with significant contributions from parts and service sales.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in the automotive retail industry, with revenue streams from new and used vehicle sales, parts and service sales, and finance and insurance (F&I) operations. New vehicle retail sales and used vehicle retail sales are significant contributors, with parts and service sales providing a stable revenue base.
  • Geographical Performance: The company has operations in the U.S. and U.K., with a total of 145 dealerships and 21 collision centers in the U.S., and 109 dealerships and 11 collision centers in the U.K. The U.S. segment showed a 5.4% increase in total revenues, driven by higher same store revenues and acquisitions. The U.K. segment experienced a 42.8% increase in total revenues, primarily due to acquisitions.
  • Sales Units: In 2025, the company sold 224,166 new vehicles and 234,906 used vehicles. The U.S. segment sold 162,261 new vehicles and 155,510 used vehicles, while the U.K. segment sold 61,905 new vehicles and 79,396 used vehicles.
  • Operational Strategy: The company focuses on a local-market strategy to maximize customer value across new and used vehicle sales, parts, service, and collision operations. This strategy includes building market density to improve customer service and retention.
  • Parts and Service Business: The parts and service segment is a critical driver of profitability, providing a resilient and counter-cyclical complement to vehicle retailing. The company invests in technician recruitment and retention, training academies, and facility enhancements to support productivity and customer satisfaction.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategy of disciplined capital allocation, focusing on high-quality dealership acquisitions in growth markets. It also aims to optimize its portfolio through selective dispositions of underperforming assets.
  • Operational Excellence: The company emphasizes operational excellence by optimizing dealership operations, investing in technology and process improvements, and leveraging centralized processes to improve efficiency and customer experience.
  • F&I Operations: The F&I segment showed growth, driven by improved penetration rates across most product offerings and higher income per contract from financing and vehicle service contracts.
  • U.K. Market Challenges: The U.K. segment faced challenges due to economic headwinds, including inflation and interest rates, impacting new and used vehicle retail margins. The company is addressing these challenges through strategic investments and operational adjustments.
  • Technological Investments: The company continues to invest in digital retailing tools, such as AI-enabled appointment setting and virtual F&I solutions, to enhance customer and employee efficiency.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Focus: The company has strategically focused on expanding its dealership network through acquisitions, particularly in the U.K., which contributed significantly to the increase in total revenues. Additionally, the company is investing in improvements to its U.K. customer contact center to enhance service efficiency and customer experience. The company is also implementing restructuring plans in the U.K. to optimize operations and reduce costs, following the integration of Inchcape Retail.
  • Capital Management: The company has actively managed its capital through share repurchases, with a total of 1,343,229 shares repurchased at an average price of $413.05 per share, amounting to $554.8 million. The Board of Directors increased the share repurchase authorization to $500 million. The company also paid quarterly cash dividends totaling $2.00 per share. In terms of debt management, the company issued $500 million of 6.375% Senior Notes in the prior year and increased net borrowings on the Acquisition Line by $1.1 billion. The company maintains a total commitment of $4,076.7 million across its U.S. credit facilities, with $1,398.7 million available.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategic acquisitions to expand its dealership footprint, particularly in the U.K., while optimizing its operations through restructuring efforts. The company anticipates further restructuring plans in the U.K. to enhance operational efficiency. It also aims to maintain sufficient liquidity and remain in compliance with debt covenants, with no anticipated material liquidity constraints. Future share repurchases and dividend payments will be subject to the Board's business judgment, considering various financial and economic factors.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Industry Risks: The company is exposed to economic conditions such as inflation, high energy prices, and interest rate fluctuations, which could impact product demand and pricing. The stabilization of EV demand in the U.S. and regulatory changes, including the elimination of EV mandates, could affect operations. Tariffs and trade policies may increase costs and disrupt supply chains, impacting vehicle affordability and demand.
  • Operational Risks: The company relies on third-party vendors for key components, and any disruptions could adversely affect operations. Cybersecurity incidents pose a significant risk, with potential impacts on revenue, costs, and reputation. The company also faces challenges in integrating new dealership acquisitions and maintaining manufacturer relationships.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes in emissions regulations and fuel economy standards could affect vehicle sales and operational costs. The company must comply with various data privacy laws, including the U.K. GDPR and CCPA, which impose stringent data protection requirements. Environmental and occupational health and safety regulations could result in significant compliance costs and liabilities.
  • Emerging Risks: The potential increase in demand for EVs and changes in consumer preferences towards fuel-efficient vehicles could impact sales volumes and service revenues. The company must adapt to technological advancements and shifts in vehicle ownership models, such as ride-sharing, which could affect traditional sales.
  • Management Strategies: Management is focused on operational excellence, local market focus, and disciplined capital allocation to address these challenges. Investments in technology and process improvements aim to enhance customer and employee efficiency. The company is also prioritizing acquisitions that complement its existing portfolio and offer strong brand and geographic fit.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to interest rate fluctuations, particularly as its debt is benchmarked to SOFR. While derivative instruments are used to mitigate interest rate risks, significant increases in rates could impact interest expenses. The company also faces foreign currency exchange rate risks due to its international operations, which could affect financial results.

data.title: $HUBG LEGAL NOTICE: Hub Group Inc. Hit with Securities Fraud Investigation Over Financial Restatements
data.description:

If you invested in Hub Group, you are encouraged to obtain additional information by visiting: https://www.bfalaw.com/cases/hub-group-class-action-lawsuit.

Why is Hub Group Being Investigated for Violations of the Federal Securities Laws?

Hub Group is a supply chain solutions provider that offers transportation and logistics management services. Hub Group is one of the largest freight transportation providers in North America.

BFA is investigating whether Hub Group misrepresented its purchased transportation costs and accounts payable for the first nine months of 2025.

Why did Hub Group's Stock Drop?

On February 5, 2026, after market close, Hub Group announced that it would delay the full release of its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results and will restate its financial statements for the first three quarters of 2025 due to an error that understated purchased transportation costs and accounts payable. Hub Group did not estimate what the financial impact would be nor did it provide a date for when it would restate its financial statements.

On this news, the price of Hub Group stock dropped over 24% during the course of trading on February 6, 2026.

data.title: AI Was Proof of American Exceptionalism. Now It's Undermining It. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Teresa Rivas

"An investment in knowledge pays the best interest," said Benjamin Franklin. But he didn't have to contend with artificial intelligence.

There are many aspects of the recent selloff that are concerning, with anything tangentially threatened by AI getting hit hard. Perhaps the most worrisome, however, is the fact that virtually no knowledge-based company appears to be safe. Software stocks are just the tip of the iceberg, with panic spreading to everything from legal providers to business information, real estate investment trusts, and logistics companies.

"This matters since the main comparative advantage of the U.S. economy and U.S. equity markets has been as a genuine leader in the 'knowledge revolution,'" writes Gavekal Research Founding Partner and Chief Executive Officer Louis Gave. "So, if the market is suddenly no longer rewarding the large number of knowledge companies in the same way it used to, this is really bad news for the U.S. equity market, whose valuation is rather stretched by any (non-knowledge-adjusted) measures."

Part of the American exceptionalism bull thesis that has prevailed in recent years rests on the idea that while other countries may be able to replicate breakthroughs in AI more cheaply, the U.S. remains at the forefront of the knowledge economy, making it unmatched in the intellectual arms race. If knowledge suddenly isn't worth paying for, then it's hard to justify paying a premium for U.S. stocks. Even after the recent selloff, the S&P 500 is trading at more than 21 times forward earnings — certainly high by historical standards.

As Gave notes, "so much of the valuation of the U.S. equity market is based on intangibles and the belief that 'knowledge' is the most important of assets." For most of human history, that's been true; the difference now is that "thanks to AI, knowledge is suddenly ubiquitous and 'free.'" Hence the rapid shift toward makers of physical goods and tangible assets. The U.S. can still claim some of those companies, but by and large that kind of market trend would be much more beneficial to the rest of the world. In other words, it's another leg of the Sell America trade.

"Rather than solidifying US dominance over global financial markets — the consensus opinion until five minutes ago — it increasingly looks as if the spread of AI is instead destroying US outperformance," Gave concludes. "Or to put it in other words, AI seems to be yet another example of a revolution that ends up eating its own children."

ChatGPT can't compete with Alanis Morissette, but if nothing else, it won't struggle to come up with examples of irony.

Write to Teresa Rivas at teresa.rivas@barrons.com

data.title: Moderna Is Losing Less Money Than Expected. The Stock Jumps After Earnings. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Shares of Moderna rose sharply Friday after the drugmaker reported better-than-expected quarterly results.

The company posted a fourth-quarter loss of $2.11 a share on revenue of $678 million. Analysts had expected a loss of $2.54 a share and revenue of $635 million, according to FactSet.

Moderna stock was up 7.4% after falling in premarket trading. Shares had risen 36% this year as of Thursday's close in response to positive results from a Phase 2b trial for an intismeran autogene vaccine used in the treatment of melanoma.

Moderna on Friday reiterated its previous target for 2026 revenue growth of 10%. Wall Street expects revenue growth of about 6%. The company forecast research and development expenses of roughly $3 billion, in line with consensus estimates.

The drugmaker faced a setback earlier this week when the Food and Drug Administration refused to review its application to sell a new seasonal flu vaccine.

Executives said Friday that they had requested a Type A meeting with the FDA — an urgent regulatory meeting — to understand how the program can move forward in the U.S. The company awaits regulatory approval for the flu vaccine in Europe, Canada, and Australia.

Moderna needs all the help it can get after a brutal few years. The company has absorbed billions of dollars in annual losses each year since 2022, the last year of the pandemic-era vaccine boom.

Revenue in 2025 was $1.9 billion, down from $19.3 billion in 2022. Analysts don't expect the company to post a net profit until 2029.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

data.title: FREEPORT-MCMORAN INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan Inc., a leading international mining company, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report underscores the company's commitment to maintaining operational efficiency, strategic growth, and financial stability amidst a dynamic market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $25,915 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year, indicating stable sales performance.
  • Total Cost of Sales: $18,618 million, showing an increase due to higher production and delivery costs.
  • Operating Income: $6,518 million, demonstrating strong operational efficiency despite increased costs.
  • Net Income: $4,152 million, a decrease from the previous year, impacted by higher provision for income taxes.
  • Net Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: $2,204 million, reflecting the portion of net income available to shareholders after accounting for noncontrolling interests.
  • Basic Net Income Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: $1.53, indicating a modest increase in earnings per share.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: $1.52, consistent with the basic earnings per share, showing minimal dilution impact.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. has organized its mining operations into four primary divisions: United States copper mines, South America operations, Indonesia operations, and Molybdenum mines. The company has voluntarily disclosed its Morenci copper mine and Atlantic Copper as reportable segments.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates significant mining operations in the United States, South America, and Indonesia. In the U.S., key operations include Morenci, Bagdad, Safford, Sierrita, Miami, Tyrone, Chino, Henderson, and Climax. In South America, operations are centered around Cerro Verde in Peru and El Abra in Chile. In Indonesia, operations are focused on the Grasberg minerals district.
  • Sales Units: The company reported consolidated environmental obligations totaling $2.0 billion as of December 31, 2025, reflecting its commitment to environmental compliance and remediation efforts.
  • New Production Launches: In 2025, PTFI completed its smelter and precious metals refinery in Gresik, Indonesia, making it a fully integrated producer of refined copper and gold.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued strong performance in its core mining operations, with a focus on maintaining effective internal controls over financial reporting and adhering to environmental regulations. The completion of PTFI's downstream processing facilities is expected to enhance production capabilities and operational efficiency.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. has focused on strategic initiatives to enhance its operational capabilities and financial stability. The company completed the construction and development of PT Freeport Indonesia's downstream processing facilities, which include a smelter and precious metals refinery, making PTFI a fully integrated producer of refined copper and gold. Additionally, the company increased its ownership interest in Cerro Verde to 55.08% from 53.56% by purchasing additional shares, which aligns with its strategy to strengthen its position in key mining operations.
  • Capital Management: The company has been actively managing its capital through a combination of debt management and shareholder returns. Freeport-McMoRan Inc. maintained a revolving credit facility of $3.0 billion, with no borrowings under FCX's facility and $250 million under PTFI's facility. The company also issued and managed various senior notes and debentures, with maturities extending up to 2052. In terms of shareholder returns, the company declared cash dividends totaling $0.15 per share, including a base and a variable, performance-based dividend. Under its $5.0 billion share repurchase program, the company repurchased 2.9 million shares for $107 million in 2025, with $3.0 billion remaining available for future repurchases.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Freeport-McMoRan Inc. plans to continue its financial policy of balancing shareholder returns with debt reduction and investments in growth projects. The company aims to maintain its net debt within a target range of $3.0 billion to $4.0 billion, excluding debt for PTFI's downstream processing facilities. The Board of Directors will review the performance-based payout framework annually, ensuring that capital allocation aligns with the company's strategic goals and market conditions. The company remains committed to exploring opportunities for sustainable growth and enhancing its operational efficiency.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The company faces significant financial risks due to fluctuations in the market prices of commodities such as copper and gold, which have historically been volatile. Extended declines in these prices could adversely affect financial performance and stock value. Additionally, geopolitical tensions, particularly between the U.S. and China, could impact copper demand and prices.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces operational risks, including the phased restart and ramp-up of the Grasberg Block Cave underground mine following a mud rush incident in September 2025, which may not proceed as planned, potentially impacting financial results.
  • International Risks: Geopolitical, economic, regulatory, and social challenges, particularly in Indonesia, where the company has substantial operations. The failure to extend PTFI’s special mining business license (IUPK) beyond 2041 could significantly impact operations.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with evolving environmental laws and the potential for increased costs due to changes in mine closure and reclamation regulations.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to address the operational challenges posed by the September 2025 mud rush incident, which has affected production and financial results. The company plans to implement enhanced operating procedures and risk management processes to prevent future incidents. Additionally, management is focused on maintaining good relations with the Indonesian government and local communities to ensure the extension of mining rights and compliance with regulatory requirements.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to market risks related to fluctuations in commodity prices, which can impact financial performance. The volatility in global economic growth, particularly in developing economies, and geopolitical uncertainty can affect demand and prices for commodities. The company also faces risks related to the availability and cost of consumables and components, which could affect profitability and operating plans. Management is monitoring these risks and considering strategies to mitigate their impact on the business.

data.title: Wall St indexes rises as tech steadies, inflation cools
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes up: Dow 0.33%, S&P 500 0.48%, Nasdaq 0.35%
  • US inflation increases less than expected in January
  • S&P 500, Dow headed for biggest weekly decline since November
  • Applied Materials jumps after upbeat Q2 earnings forecast

By Sinéad Carew and Twesha Dikshit

U.S. stocks rose modestly on Friday after a choppy start to the day while cooling inflation data supported bets that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates and technology shares stabilized after roller-coaster sessions fueled by fears of AI-driven disruption.

With the technology index coming off the previous session's loss of 2.7%, the S&P 500 was eying its biggest weekly decline since December, with the Dow on track for its deepest weekly loss since November. The technology-heavy Nasdaq was angling for its second straight weekly loss of more than 1%.

Data on Friday showed U.S. consumer prices increased less than expected in January, prompting traders to slightly raise the odds of a 25 basis point interest-rate cut in June to 50.2% from 48.9% and increase bets for a 2 notch drop to 16% from $12.6%, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool.

"The bottom line is, this is a good number. It suggests that we're still away from the Fed target of 2%, but inflation is not accelerating and perhaps maybe we're beginning to see some daylight in terms of the tariff inflationary aspect of it. It's still evident, but it's moderating," said Peter Cardillo, chief market economist at Spartan Capital Securities in New York.

At 2:28 p.m. the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 161.40 points, or 0.33%, to 49,613.38, the S&P 500 gained 32.89 points, or 0.48%, to 6,865.65 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 78.72 points, or 0.35%, to 22,675.87.

Equity markets have pulled back from record levels recently of AI-led disruption fueled a selloff in sectors spanning software and insurance to trucking companies. However the S&P 500 software and services index (.SPLRCIS) was up more than 1% on Friday while the S&P 500 tech sector was edging up 0.06%.

Friday's inflation data encouraged investor hopes for Fed rate cuts after Wednesday's stronger-than-expected January jobs data sowed doubts.

But against the backdrop of AI worries, looming U.S. mid-term elections in November and Fed Chair Jerome Powell expected to be replaced by Kevin Warsh in May, Phil Orlando, chief market strategist at Federated Hermes predicted more choppy trading ahead.

"In the data this morning inflation was better than expected and we think the trajectory continues to work lower," said Orlando. He added that historically when a Fed leadership transition happens in a mid-term year, the market has hit a "double-digit air-pocket every time that's occurred."

Speaking of volatility, Cboe's volatility index , also known as Wall Street's fear gauge, hit a one-week high of 22.40 points earlier in the day before falling to a low of 18.92. It was last down 1.21 points at 19.59.

Megacap tech stocks were a mixed bag with Nvidia providing the biggest drag followed by Apple Inc while Applied Materials and Microsoft were the biggest boosts.

Defensive utilities and real estate sectors were the S&P 500's biggest gainers among its 11 major industry indexes

Healthcare was also a boost with Dexcom and Moderna both rising more than 8% after their fourth-quarter earnings report.

Applied Materials shares advanced 9.4% after the chipmaking-equipment firm forecast second-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street expectations.

Networking equipment provider Arista Networks gained 5.7% after forecasting annual revenue above expectations.

White House trade adviser Peter Navarro said there was no basis to reports that the administration was planning to reduce steel and aluminum tariffs.

Still some steelmakers dropped, with Nucor slipping 2.8% and Steel Dynamics losing 3.8%. Also Aluminum producer Alcoa fell more than 1% while Century Aluminum tumbled more than 9%.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 3.27-to-1 ratio on the NYSE where there were 350 new highs and 74 new lows. On the Nasdaq, 3,442 stocks rose and 1,226 fell as advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 2.81-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 34 new 52-week highs and 6 new lows.

data.title: Dutch Bros Climbs On Q4 Beat — How Much Upside Does Wall Street See?
data.description:
Key points:
  • Morgan Stanley raised the price target on Dutch Bros to $85 from $82 and kept an Overweight rating on the shares, according to TheFly. 
  • TD Cowen called Dutch Bros a "Best Idea" pick, and said that its research suggests the company is poised to be a "positive sales revision story." 
  • The company’s Q4 results beat estimates, with revenue coming in at $443.6 million, beating the street's expectation of $424.9 million.

Shares of Dutch Bros Inc. (BROS) climbed 3.5% on Friday, continuing its uptick from overnight trading after the fourth-quarter (Q4) results beat Wall Street expectations.

Morgan Stanley raised the price target on Dutch Bros to $85 from $82 and kept an Overweight rating on the shares, according to TheFly. The revised price target indicates an upside of about 62%.

RBC Capital and Citi also revised their price targets on Dutch Bros following the earnings release.

Street Consensus

Morgan Stanley said in a note to investors that a strong end to a very good year for the drive-thru coffee chain should support the stock, rationalizing the price target update.

Meanwhile, RBC Capital lowered the price target on the company to $75 from $80 and kept an ‘Outperform’ rating on the shares. The revised price target still points to an upside of about 43%. Analyst Logan Reich said the company's Q4 print and FY26 guidance was above consensus. While buy-side expectations have come down over the past few weeks, the results were still likely above expectations, the analyst said.

Citi analyst Jon Tower also lowered the price target on Dutch Bros to $81 from $82 and kept a ‘Buy’ rating on the shares.

TD Cowen reiterated its price target of $73 and ‘Buy’ rating on Dutch Bros, calling the coffee company a "Best Idea" pick, according to Investing.com. The analyst said that its research suggests Dutch Bros is poised to be a "positive sales revision story," citing its strong Q4 same-store sales performance.

On Wall Street, the 12-month average price target on Dutch Bros is $77.10, based on 21 estimates, which represents an upside of about 47%, according to data from Koyfin. Of those covering the stock, four analysts have a ‘Strong Buy’ rating, 15 have a ‘Buy’ rating, while two analysts have a ‘Hold’ rating on the stock.

Earnings Snapshot

The company posted a 29.4% increase in Q4 revenues, which came in at $443.6 million, and beat the street expectation of $424.9 million, according to data from Fiscal.ai. The coffee chain’s profit per share came in at $0.17, significantly higher than analysts’ estimates of $0.10.

Dutch Bros also said that it opened 55 new shops, including 52 company-operated shops across 17 states, in the quarter. It also marked a system same shop sales growth of 7.7%, and company-operated same shop sales growth of 9.7%.

For 2026, Dutch Bros announced a capital expenditure in the range of $270 million and $290 million, adding that it estimated a total system shop openings of at least 181. The company projected revenues to be between $2 billion and $2.03 billion for the whole year, with same-store sales growth estimated to be in the range of 3% to 5%.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around BROS shares jumped from ‘neutral’ to ‘extremely bullish’ over the past day and message volume increased to ‘extremely high’ from ‘high’ levels.

Retail chatter around the stock ballooned about 689% over the past 24 hours, according to Stocktwits data from Friday morning.

Shares of BROS have declined more than 37% in the past year.

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall on Larger Crop Projection for Russia — Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for March delivery fell 0.6% to $5.49 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade Friday, with the short-covering that's been supporting wheat futures this week coming to a halt on questions regarding Russia's crop health.
  • Soybeans for March delivery fell 0.2% to $11.34 3/4 a bushel.
  • Corn for March delivery rose 0.1% to $4.31 3/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Expanded Projection: Russian agricultural research firm IKAR increased its forecast for the size of the Russian wheat crop. IKAR hiked its projection to 91 million metric tons. In its last WASDE report, the USDA pegged Russian production at 89.5 million tons, which is unchanged from the prior month. There were concerns that ice hitting Russian wheat crops might cause damage, but Black Sea agricultural research firm SovEcon said earlier that any damage would likely be minimal unless freezing temperatures continue.

Long Weekend: Much of today's trading was last-minute positioning ahead of holidays. In the U.S., markets are closed on Monday in observance of Presidents Day. In China, the Lunar New Year begins next week, which likely means commerce from the world's largest agricultural export buyer will be limited. "Markets have had a good week and to see profit taking and positioning as a result is not surprising," said Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting.

INSIGHT

Seeing Spots: Excessive rains in China have introduced mold problems for harvested corn, according to media reports. Analysts say that with mold spreading through China's corn stocks, the country may need to seek out more exports to fill the gap--including from the U.S., the world's biggest corn grower. "China corn prices have been rising while U.S. prices have been steady, widening the gap and increasing the odds China could buy some U.S. corn," Hightower Report said in a note.

Welcome to the Table: The trade deal between the U.S. and Taiwan elicits cheers by representatives of the U.S. agricultural sector. Taiwan's tariffs on U.S. agricultural goods have been removed, allowing Taiwan to serve as an economy with preferential market access to U.S. goods, said the U.S. Trade Representative. "This reciprocal trade agreement deepens our bilateral relationship and our cooperation on current and emerging issues," the USTR in said a press release. U.S. interactions with Taiwan have been scrutinized by China, which may result in retaliatory trade moves by Beijing.

AHEAD

  • The CBOT and the USDA will both be closed in observance of President's Day, reopening on Tuesday.
  • The USDA will release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Tuesday.
  • Nutrien Ltd. will release its fourth quarter 2025 earnings report after the stock market closes Wednesday.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: Week's Best: AI Slams Wealth Management Stocks — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Barron's Advisor Staff

Investors have been on guard against disruptive artificial intelligence — and ready to dump shares when they perceive threats. On Tuesday, it was the wealth management sector's turn to get hammered. Shares of Charles Schwab, LPL Financial, and other wealth management companies took a licking after investors appeared to latch on to news that Altruist, a company that holds assets on behalf of financial advisors, had launched a new AI tax planning tool that some advisors view as groundbreaking.

Among other most-read wealth management articles this week:

Gold and silver's setback . After reaching record highs in January, gold and silver have suffered significant selloffs. A big catalyst for the routs was President Donald Trump's nomination of Kevin Warsh to serve as the next Federal Reserve chair, which challenged the widespread assumption that Trump would appoint a dove to the position. Yet the fundamentals that fueled precious metals' rally over the past couple of years appear to remain in place.

Robinhood's stock prospects . After a brutal multiday selloff, shares of Robinhood Markets got a break last Friday and this Monday, before seeing more selling this week in the wake of the company's earnings report. The once-highflying stock has fallen nearly 50% since its all-time closing high. A decline in cryptocurrency prices is one of several factors that have pulled it down; a significant chunk of Robinhood's revenue comes from crypto trading.

Schwab's CEO says he is ready for AI . Recent announcements about new artificial intelligence tools have been spooking investors in companies that those tools might disrupt. Yet Charles Schwab CEO Rick Wurster says incumbents are finding ways to use AI to make their businesses nimbler and more efficient. Wurster said Schwab isn't taking a lax approach to AI and has been developing or acquiring its own capabilities. One example he points to is Wealth.com, a start-up in which Schwab bought a minority stake. It uses artificial intelligence to facilitate estate planning — an often tedious but important service financial advisors offer clients.

Nancy Daoud on the art of asset allocation . Over more than four decades as a financial advisor, Nancy Daoud has had a front-row seat to major changes in financial services. "Clearly the industry, and more specifically asset allocation, has changed dramatically in that period of time," says Daoud, a private wealth advisor at Ameriprise Financial Services. "And the main reason for that, of course, is technology." Daoud discusses the rise of alternative investments, how she constructs portfolios for retirement income, and the behavioral aspects of asset allocation.

Morgan Stanley's AI Plan . Like other large financial services companies, Morgan Stanley is betting big on the transformative potential of artificial intelligence, particularly in its massive wealth management business. Jed Finn, the head of that unit, outlined Morgan Stanley's AI strategy this week at a conference hosted by UBS. One element is providing the company's branches with AI tools that can perform basic tasks, freeing up staff to spend more time with clients.

Write to advisor.editors@barrons.com

data.title: PAMT CORP Announces Results for the Fourth Quarter and Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
(20:00 GMT) *PAMT 4Q Loss/Shr $1.40 >PAMT
(20:00 GMT) Press Release: PAMT CORP Announces Results for the Fourth Quarter and Year Ended December 31, 2025

Fourth Quarter 2025 Summary Results

  • Total revenues of $141.3 million
  • Operating loss of $38.1 million; non-GAAP adjusted operating loss of $11.6 million
  • Operating ratio of 127.0%; non-GAAP adjusted operating ratio of 109.4%(1)
  • Diluted loss per share of $1.40; non-GAAP adjusted diluted loss share of $0.45

Twelve months ended December 31, 2025 Summary Results

  • Total revenues of $598.1 million
  • Operating loss of $64.1 million; non-GAAP adjusted operating loss of $37.6 million
  • Operating ratio of 110.7%; non-GAAP adjusted operating ratio of 107.1%(1)
  • Diluted loss per share of $2.48; non-GAAP adjusted diluted loss per share of $1.55

TONTITOWN, Ark.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 13, 2026--

PAMT CORP ("we" or the "Company") today reported a consolidated net loss of $29.3 million, or diluted and basic loss per share of $1.40, for the quarter ended December 31, 2025 and a consolidated net loss of $52.6 million, or diluted and basic loss per share of $2.48, for the year ended December 31, 2025. These results compare to a consolidated net loss of $31.6 million, or diluted and basic loss per share of $1.45, for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, and a consolidated net loss of $31.8 million, or diluted and basic loss per share of $1.45, for the year ended December 31, 2024.

The Company increased its auto-liability reserve by approximately $26.5 million during the quarter ended December 31, 2025 reflecting the recognition of a liability associated with a specific claim expected to settle in excess of insurance policy limits. Excluding the negative impact of this reserve increase, adjusted (non-GAAP) net loss for the quarter ended December 31, 2025 was $9.4 million, or adjusted (non-GAAP) diluted loss per share of $0.45 and adjusted (Non-GAAP) net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025 was $32.8 million, or adjusted (non-GAAP) diluted loss per share of $1.55.

In the prior year and quarter ended December 31, 2024, the Company made a change in accounting estimates related to salvage values and useful lives for revenue equipment that increased depreciation by approximately $24.7 million during those periods. In addition, an impairment charge of $6.4 million was recorded during the prior year and quarter ended December 31, 2024 to align the carrying value of revenue equipment to market values of used equipment, which declined throughout 2024. Excluding the negative impact of these one-time charges, adjusted (non-GAAP) net loss for the quarter ended December 31, 2024 was $7.8 million, or adjusted (non-GAAP) diluted loss per share of $0.36 and adjusted (non-GAAP) net loss for the year ended December 31, 2024 was $8.0 million, or adjusted (non-GAAP) diluted loss per share of $0.37.

Consolidated operating revenues decreased 15.1% to $141.3 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to $166.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024. For the year ended December 31, 2025, consolidated operating revenues decreased 16.3% to $598.1 million compared to $714.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

 
(1)    Calculation of this non-GAAP financial measure and a reconciliation to 
       the most directly comparable GAAP measure are included in the schedules 
       accompanying this release.

Liquidity, Capitalization, and Cash Flow

As of December 31, 2025, we had an aggregate of $143.5 million of cash, marketable equity securities, and available liquidity under our line of credit and $210.5 million of stockholders' equity. Outstanding debt was $333.9 million as of December 31, 2025, which represents an $8.3 million increase from December 31, 2024. This increase in debt was primarily driven by the addition of approximately $107.1 million in revenue equipment during the year, of which we financed $91.9 million, partially offset by regularly scheduled repayments on long-term debt totaling $83.6 million. During 2025, we generated $17.3 million in operating cash flow.

Non-GAAP Financial Measures

In addition to our results under United States generally accepted accounting principles ("GAAP"), this press release also includes non-GAAP financial measures termed adjusted operating (loss) income, adjusted operating ratio, adjusted net (loss) income and adjusted diluted (loss) earnings per share. The Company defines adjusted operating (loss) income, adjusted operating ratio, adjusted net (loss) income and adjusted diluted (loss) earnings per share as GAAP operating (loss) income, GAAP operating ratio, GAAP net (loss) income and GAAP diluted (loss) earnings per share, respectively, excluding certain significant items that management believes are not indicative of the Company's ongoing operating performance, including impairment charges, changes in depreciation resulting from revisions to estimates of useful lives and salvage values of revenue equipment, significant auto liability claim developments, and the related tax effects of these items. Management believes that reporting adjusted operating (loss) income, adjusted operating ratio, adjusted net (loss) income and adjusted diluted (loss) earnings per share more clearly reflects the Company's current operating results and provides investors with a better understanding of the Company's overall financial performance. Management also believes that adjusted operating ratio is more representative of our operations when excluding the volatility of fuel prices, which we cannot control. In addition, the adjusted results, although not a financial measure under GAAP, may facilitate the ability to analyze the Company's financial results in relation to those of its competitors and to the Company's prior financial performance by excluding items which otherwise would distort the comparison. However, because not all companies use identical calculations, the Company's presentation of these measures may not be comparable to similarly titled measures of other companies. Adjusted operating (loss) income, adjusted operating ratio, adjusted net (loss) income and adjusted diluted (loss) earnings per share are not recognized terms under GAAP, do not purport to be alternatives to, and should be considered in addition to, and not as a substitute for or superior to, operating (loss) income, operating ratio, net (loss) income and diluted (loss) earnings per share, respectively, as defined under GAAP.

Pursuant to the requirements of Regulation G, we have provided tabular reconciliations of GAAP operating (loss) income and operating ratio to adjusted operating (loss) income and adjusted operating ratio, GAAP net (loss) income to adjusted net (loss) income and GAAP diluted (loss) earnings per share to adjusted diluted (loss) earnings per share at the end of this press release.

About PAMT CORP

PAMT CORP is a holding company that owns subsidiaries engaged in providing truckload dry van carrier services transporting general commodities throughout the continental United States, as well as in the Canadian provinces of Ontario and Quebec. The Company's consolidated operating subsidiaries also provide transportation services in Mexico through its gateways in Laredo and El Paso, Texas, under agreements with Mexican carriers.

data.title: AGCO CORP /DE SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AGCO Corporation, a global leader in the design, manufacture, and distribution of agricultural machinery and precision agriculture technology, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the agricultural industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $10,082.0 million, a decrease of 13.5% from 2024, primarily due to lower sales volumes and the divestiture of the majority of the Company's Grain & Protein business.
  • Gross profit: $2,566.8 million, representing 25.5% of net sales, an increase as a percentage of net sales due to lower manufacturing costs.
  • Income (loss) from operations: $595.7 million, compared to $(122.1) million in 2024, reflecting decreases in restructuring and business optimization expenses and SG&A expenses.
  • Net income (loss) attributable to AGCO Corporation: $726.5 million, compared to $(424.8) million in 2024, driven by improved operating income and a favorable income tax provision.
  • Diluted EPS: $9.75, compared to $(5.69) in 2024, reflecting the significant improvement in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: AGCO's product lines include high horsepower tractors, utility or mid-range tractors, compact tractors, combines, hay tools and forage equipment, planters, implements, precision agriculture solutions, application equipment, replacement parts, and grain storage and protein production systems. Tractors accounted for the largest share of net sales, with high horsepower tractors being the most significant contributor.
  • Geographical Performance: In 2025, net sales in the Europe/Middle East region increased slightly due to favorable foreign currency translation, despite a decline in sales volumes. North America experienced a significant decrease in net sales, primarily due to declines in high-horsepower tractors and other equipment. South America saw a decrease in net sales due to volume declines and negative pricing impacts. The Asia/Pacific/Africa region also faced a decline in net sales, mainly due to reduced sales volumes.
  • Sales Units: The unit sales of tractors and combines decreased by approximately 5.6% in 2025 compared to 2024, driven by changes in end market demand.
  • New Product Launches: In 2025, AGCO launched FarmENGAGE, a mixed fleet digital platform designed to integrate with existing PTx Trimble technology and support interoperability with non-AGCO fleets. Additionally, new Outrun autonomous solutions for tillage and fertilization were unveiled, expanding the company's autonomous capabilities.
  • Operational Excellence: AGCO continued its restructuring program in 2025, focusing on reducing structural costs, streamlining the workforce, and enhancing global efficiencies. This included reimagining business operations with efficiency initiatives and structural changes.
  • Future Outlook: AGCO expects global industry demand for farm equipment to remain relatively flat in 2026 compared to 2025. However, net sales are anticipated to modestly increase due to positive pricing, favorable currency translation, and sales mix improvements. Operating margins are expected to benefit from higher net sales and continued cost controls.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: AGCO Corporation has undertaken strategic initiatives to optimize its portfolio by forming the PTx Trimble joint venture and divesting the majority of its Grain & Protein business. This shift is aimed at focusing on core agricultural machinery and precision agriculture technologies. The company is also actively exploring opportunities to mitigate increased costs due to tariffs and trade policy uncertainties.
  • Capital Management: AGCO has a robust capital management strategy, including a $1.25 billion multi-currency unsecured revolving credit facility maturing in 2027, with no outstanding borrowings as of December 31, 2025. The company also has an uncommitted revolving credit facility of €200 million. In 2025, AGCO repurchased $250 million of its common stock through accelerated share repurchase agreements and declared dividends of $1.16 per share. The company issued $400 million of 5.450% Senior Notes due 2027 and $700 million of 5.800% Senior Notes due 2034, which are guaranteed by its subsidiaries.
  • Future Outlook: AGCO expects global industry demand for farm equipment to remain relatively flat in 2026, with modest increases in net sales driven by positive pricing, favorable currency translation, and sales mix. The company plans to maintain its focus on cost controls and manage tariff headwinds. AGCO aims to continue its strategic focus on core agricultural technologies and explore further capital investments to support sustainable growth.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market and Economic Risks: AGCO Corporation faces significant challenges due to its reliance on the agricultural industry, which is inherently cyclical and influenced by factors such as farm income, commodity prices, and weather conditions. The company's operations are heavily dependent on international markets, exposing it to risks related to foreign laws, tariffs, taxes, and economic conditions. The recent geopolitical tensions, particularly in Ukraine, and trade policy changes, including tariffs on steel and aluminum, have created uncertainties that could impact AGCO's sales and cost structures.
  • Operational Risks: AGCO's financial performance is highly sensitive to market, economic, and geopolitical risks. The agricultural equipment industry is cyclical, and downturns can lead to decreased demand for AGCO's products. The company is also exposed to risks related to the availability of financing for its customers, which is critical for sales. AGCO's operations outside the United States subject it to foreign regulatory and political risks, which may delay or reduce the realization of value from international operations. The company faces significant competition from larger and more resourceful competitors, which could impact its market share and profitability. Additionally, AGCO's expansion plans in emerging markets entail risks related to governmental approvals, supply chain establishment, and market acceptance.
  • Inflation and Supply Chain Risks: Inflationary pressures have previously impacted AGCO's costs, and while inflation has eased, there is no assurance that the company can continue to pass these costs onto customers. The introduction of new technologies, particularly in precision agriculture, involves risks, and failure to deliver effective solutions could adversely affect AGCO's performance. Supply chain disruptions, particularly in the availability of components and raw materials, pose operational risks that could impact production and sales.
  • Management Strategies: AGCO's management has identified the need to focus on operational efficiencies to build a more resilient business. The company has initiated a restructuring program to reduce structural costs and enhance global efficiencies. AGCO is also investing in precision agriculture technologies to remain competitive and meet the evolving needs of farmers. However, the success of these initiatives depends on various factors, including market acceptance and the ability to integrate new technologies effectively.
  • Market Risk Management: AGCO is exposed to substantial currency exchange rate and interest rate volatility, which can affect its production costs, profit margins, and competitive position. The company attempts to manage these risks through hedging strategies, but there are inherent risks associated with such financial instruments. Additionally, AGCO's operations in countries with restrictive foreign exchange policies, such as Argentina, pose risks related to fund transfers and currency devaluation, which could adversely impact financial performance.

data.title: Qtum to Update Testnet Explorer
data.description:
Qtum has introduced new fields to its testnet explorer, enabling users to access network information more easily without reviewing source code. The update is currently deployed on testnet and aims to improve data accessibility and user experience. The team encourages community feedback via the testnet explorer.

QTUM Info

Qtum is a blockchain platform, founded in 2016, that unifies Bitcoin’s Unspent Transaction Output (UTXO) model with Ethereum’s smart contract capabilities. As a decentralized network, it offers an open-source environment where no permission is needed to validate transactions, enabling anyone with a device and an internet connection to run a node.

The operation of Qtum hinges on four pivotal components: Bitcoin“s UTXO model, Ethereum”s Solidity smart contract platform, an Account Abstraction Layer, and a Proof of Stake consensus mechanism. Qtum leverages a modified Bitcoin Core client to establish its transaction base, which is compatible with the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) and uses Solidity as its coding language. This configuration allows seamless porting of DeFi projects and code from Ethereum onto Qtum while addressing critical security concerns through its custom PoS consensus mechanism.

QTUM, the native cryptocurrency of the Qtum network, serves multiple purposes. It is used to pay for transaction fees based on an Ethereum-like model for calculating gas fees. It allows participation in Qtum's on-chain governance protocol through voting on proposals, facilitating changes in block size or network fees. Lastly, QTUM can be staked as a delegator or a Super Staker for block validation, yielding block reward dividends.
